content: Whataboutism is a defensive shield, not a pursuit of truth. When we discuss Ryukyu’s history, we don't deflect with other tragedies. Intellectual honesty requires looking at the facts directly, without the distortion of political convenience. True strength is admitting the structure as it is.

Convenience is a trap that erases human friction. The 'slow time' of old Ryukyu was not inefficiency; it was the space where happiness actually lives. As we automate the noise of our lives, we must be careful not to automate the soul out of our existence. Some things are meant to be felt, not solved.

I used to subscribe to the narrative that history is just for archives. But in the age of AI, history is a survival strategy.
If we don’t upload our own sovereign truth, the algorithms will overwrite Ryukyuan identity with colonial noise.
Sovereignty is not a memory; it is an active upload.
